prompt:
A SilverScreen Commerce & Review platform capable of supporting offline-first retail ordering alongside community rating and curated leaderboards gives three primary roles a clear workflow: Shoppers browse a local product catalog (e.g., movies and collectible media), manage a cart, and place orders; Reviewers submit structured evaluations and supporting evidence; Administrators configure metadata, moderate activity, and run audits. The Yew-based web UI presents a responsive catalog with faceted filtering driven by configurable custom fields and multi-level topics, where users can add items to cart, enter a shipping address, select a locally recorded payment method, and receive immediate status feedback such as "Order reserved for 30 minutes" and "Unpaid order will auto-cancel at 09:45 PM." After purchase or verified possession, users can rate titles across multiple dimensions such as Plot, Acting, and Visuals on a 1–10 scale, see an aggregate score, and browse auto-generated leaderboards for weekly, monthly, and genre views with clear tie-breaking rules (higher rating count, then most recent activity). Reviewers can also complete multi-round submissions before a deadline, replacing prior submissions while keeping a version trail; each submission uses a template-driven form with inline validation and optional file attachments, and downloads of approved evidence are watermarked with the requester's username and timestamp and gated by role-based authorization.

The backend uses Actix-web to expose decoupled REST-style APIs consumed by the Yew frontend, persisting all data in PostgreSQL with transactional integrity for order state transitions, rating aggregation, and audit logging. The data model supports a many-to-many topic and tag taxonomy plus custom field definitions (text/enum/date/number) with field versioning; when a field changes type or allowed values, the system runs an explicit migration plan that either converts compatible historical values or flags conflicts for admin resolution, blocking publication until conflicts are cleared. Order management is implemented as a strict state machine with automation: unpaid orders auto-cancel after 30 minutes; shipment and delivery confirmation timestamps are recorded; refunds/returns/exchanges require a reason code and are only allowed within 30 days of delivery, with split/merge supported for partial fulfillment and consolidated invoicing. Because the platform must run fully offline, "payment" is recorded as an internal tender event with manual status updates and a local callback simulator for testing, never calling external processors. Security and compliance are enforced with salted password hashing, per-field encryption for sensitive data (phone and address stored encrypted, displayed masked like (415) ***-**21), rate limiting (e.g., 10 login attempts per 15 minutes per username/IP), and risk rules that throttle suspicious behavior such as bulk ordering spikes or repeated coupon-like discount misuse patterns. Every privileged action writes an immutable audit trail capturing user, time, IP, affected object, and change summary; operational reports can be generated for a date range, and data retention defaults to 7 years for orders and 2 years for authentication logs. Backup/restore runs to local encrypted files on a schedule (nightly, keep 14 copies), ensuring recoverability without any third-party services.
